title: crepis tectorum subsp. nigrescens
intro: Crepis tectorum subsp. nigrescens is a subspecies of the hawk's beard or rough hawk's beard plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for various gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Crepis tectorum subsp. nigrescens include its potential to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, reduce inflammation, and even act as a natural laxative. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
